source:
## 10. OpenCRE Discovery: External Linkage Ground Truth

**Motivation.** The v7c classifier scores 0.00 F1 on the EQUIVALENT tier because the expert-annotated corpus contains only 16 EQUIVALENT pairs out of 4,059 total—far too few for the model to learn any decision boundary. We need an external source of pair-level semantic links, and the [Open Common Requirement Enumeration](https://www.opencre.org/) (OpenCRE) project provides exactly that. OpenCRE is a community-maintained taxonomy that maps security controls across dozens of frameworks through a shared set of Common Requirement Enumerations (CREs). Every CRE acts as a hub: if two controls link to the same CRE, they address the same requirement. If they link through one or two intermediate CREs, the relationship is weaker but still present.

The critical prior is straightforward: **if OpenCRE links two controls through any path, those controls are never UNRELATED.** Hop distance (0, 1, or 2) modulates the expected label from EQUIVALENT/RELATED down to PARTIAL, but zero-hop pairs that share a CRE directly represent the strongest external evidence of semantic equivalence available in any public dataset.

This section loads the 13,519 OpenCRE-derived pairs, profiles their hop-distance distribution and framework coverage, and establishes the contamination firewall that prevents any pair already in our expert corpus from leaking into the augmentation set.

# 10.1 — Load OpenCRE pairs and profile the dataset
from collections import Counter

ocre_path = REPO / "data" / "opencre" / "opencre_pairs.jsonl"
ocre_pairs = []
with open(ocre_path) as f:
    for line in f:
        ocre_pairs.append(json.loads(line))

ocre_df = pd.DataFrame(ocre_pairs)

# Distinct frameworks appearing in either source or target position
ocre_fws = sorted(set(ocre_df["source_framework"]) | set(ocre_df["target_framework"]))

# Top 5 frameworks by mention count (source + target)
fw_counts = Counter()
for _, row in ocre_df.iterrows():
    fw_counts[row["source_framework"]] += 1
    fw_counts[row["target_framework"]] += 1
top5 = fw_counts.most_common(5)

# Hop distance distribution
hop_dist = Counter(ocre_df["gap_penalty"])

print(f"OpenCRE pairs loaded:  {len(ocre_df):,}")
print(f"Distinct frameworks:   {len(ocre_fws)}")
print(f"\nTop 5 frameworks (by mention count):")
for fw, cnt in top5:
    print(f"  {fw:45s} {cnt:,}")
print(f"\nHop-distance distribution:")
for hop in sorted(hop_dist):
    print(f"  Hop {hop}: {hop_dist[hop]:,}")

# Figure 10.1 — Hop distance bar chart
hop_labels = ["Hop 0\n(same CRE)", "Hop 1\n(adjacent CRE)", "Hop 2\n(2-hop path)"]
hop_values = [hop_dist[0], hop_dist[1], hop_dist[2]]
hop_colors = ["#2a9d8f", "#e9c46a", "#e76f51"]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
bars = ax.bar(hop_labels, hop_values, color=hop_colors, edgecolor="white", width=0.55)

for bar, val in zip(bars, hop_values):
    ax.text(
        bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
        bar.get_height() + 120,
        f"{val:,}",
        ha="center", va="bottom", fontweight="bold", fontsize=11,
    )

ax.set_ylabel("Number of pairs")
ax.set_title("Figure 10.1 \u00b7 OpenCRE pair distribution by hop distance")
ax.set_ylim(0, max(hop_values) * 1.15)
sns.despine()

fig.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(REPO / "report" / "figures" / "fig10_1_hop_distance.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

**Interpreting hop distance.** Hop 0 pairs (5,242) share the same CRE directly—these carry the strongest semantic signal and map to EQUIVALENT or RELATED labels. Hop 1 pairs (3,129) are connected through one intermediate CRE, corresponding to RELATED or PARTIAL overlap. Hop 2 pairs (5,148) traverse two CRE links; the relationship is real but noisier, so these map to PARTIAL at most. The scoring schema deliberately downgrades confidence with each additional hop to avoid injecting false EQUIVALENT labels into the training set.

# Figure 10.2 — Framework coverage: OpenCRE vs. our corpus

# Count total mentions per framework (source + target)
fw_mention = Counter()
for _, row in ocre_df.iterrows():
    fw_mention[row["source_framework"]] += 1
    fw_mention[row["target_framework"]] += 1

top12 = fw_mention.most_common(12)
top12_names = [t[0] for t in top12]
top12_counts = [t[1] for t in top12]

# Which of our 9 frameworks appear in OpenCRE?
overlap_map = {"mitre_atlas": "mitre_atlas", "owasp_ai_exchange": "owasp_ai_exchange"}
overlap_set = set(overlap_map.keys())

bar_colors = ["#2a9d8f" if fw in overlap_set else "#6e7681" for fw in top12_names]

fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5), gridspec_kw={"width_ratios": [2.2, 1]})

# Left panel (10.2A): horizontal bar chart
y_pos = range(len(top12_names))
ax1.barh(y_pos, top12_counts, color=bar_colors, edgecolor="white")
ax1.set_yticks(y_pos)
ax1.set_yticklabels(top12_names, fontsize=9)
ax1.invert_yaxis()
ax1.set_xlabel("Mention count (source + target)")
ax1.set_title("Figure 10.2A \u00b7 Top 12 OpenCRE frameworks by pair frequency")

# Annotate overlapping frameworks
for i, fw in enumerate(top12_names):
    if fw in overlap_set:
        ax1.annotate(
            "\u2605 in our corpus",
            xy=(top12_counts[i], i),
            xytext=(top12_counts[i] + max(top12_counts) * 0.05, i),
            fontsize=8, fontweight="bold", color="#2a9d8f", va="center",
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", lw=1, color="black"),
        )

sns.despine(ax=ax1)

# Right panel (10.2B): text summary
ax2.axis("off")

# Build summary text
our_fws_in_ocre = sorted(overlap_set)
ocre_only = sorted(set(ocre_fws) - set(FRAMEWORKS))

# Count pairs where at least one framework is in our corpus
overlap_pairs = ocre_df[
    ocre_df["source_framework"].isin(overlap_set) | ocre_df["target_framework"].isin(overlap_set)
].shape[0]

summary_lines = [
    "Coverage Summary",
    "\u2500" * 30,
    f"Our corpus frameworks:      {len(FRAMEWORKS)}",
    f"OpenCRE frameworks:          {len(ocre_fws)}",
    f"Frameworks in common:        {len(our_fws_in_ocre)}",
    f"  \u2022 {', '.join(our_fws_in_ocre)}",
    "",
    f"Total OpenCRE pairs:         {len(ocre_df):,}",
    f"Pairs touching our corpus:   {overlap_pairs:,}",
    "",
    "OpenCRE-only frameworks:",
]
for fw in ocre_only[:10]:
    summary_lines.append(f"  \u2022 {fw}")
if len(ocre_only) > 10:
    summary_lines.append(f"  ... +{len(ocre_only) - 10} more")

ax2.text(
    0.05, 0.95, "\n".join(summary_lines),
    transform=ax2.transAxes, fontsize=9, verticalalignment="top",
    fontfamily="monospace",
    bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.5", facecolor="#f0f0f0", edgecolor="#cccccc"),
)
ax2.set_title("Figure 10.2B \u00b7 Corpus overlap", fontsize=11)

fig.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(REPO / "report" / "figures" / "fig10_2_opencre_coverage.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

# Figure 10.3 — Contamination firewall: removing expert-corpus overlaps
v8_assembly = json.loads(
    (REPO / "runs" / "v8_diagnosis" / "v8_data_assembly.json").read_text()
)

total = v8_assembly["opencre_total"]
contaminated = v8_assembly["contaminated"]
clean = v8_assembly["clean"]
pct_removed = contaminated / total * 100

print(f"OpenCRE candidate pairs:  {total:,}")
print(f"Contaminated (in expert): {contaminated}  ({pct_removed:.1f}%)")
print(f"Clean pairs retained:     {clean:,}")

# Waterfall / funnel bar chart
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))

labels = ["OpenCRE\ncandidates", "Removed\n(in expert set)", "Clean pairs\nretained"]
values = [total, contaminated, clean]
colors = ["#2a9d8f", "#e76f51", "#264653"]

bars = ax.bar(labels, values, color=colors, edgecolor="white", width=0.5)

# Count labels above each bar
for bar, val in zip(bars, values):
    ax.text(
        bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
        bar.get_height() + 80,
        f"{val:,}",
        ha="center", va="bottom", fontweight="bold", fontsize=11,
    )

# Annotate the removal with an arrow
ax.annotate(
    f"{pct_removed:.1f}% removed",
    xy=(1, contaminated + 50),
    xytext=(1.55, total * 0.55),
    fontsize=10, fontweight="bold", color="#e76f51",
    arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", lw=1, color="black"),
    ha="center",
)

ax.set_ylabel("Number of pairs")
ax.set_title("Figure 10.3 \u00b7 Contamination firewall: expert-corpus overlap removal")
ax.set_ylim(0, total * 1.2)
sns.despine()

fig.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(REPO / "report" / "figures" / "fig10_3_contamination_firewall.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

> **Plain English:** OpenCRE is a public taxonomy that maps security controls across 20 frameworks through shared requirement identifiers (CREs). We extracted 13,519 control pairs linked by 0–2 CRE hops and applied a contamination firewall that removed 34 pairs (0.5%) already present in the expert-annotated corpus, yielding 6,200 clean augmentation pairs. Only two of our nine frameworks—MITRE ATLAS and OWASP AI Exchange—appear in OpenCRE, but the signal transfers because the classification task (gauging semantic overlap between control descriptions) is domain-agnostic: the model learns from text similarity, not from framework-specific structure.
